claim_raw: Many plan subcommand handlers call bare load_plan() ignoring the --state-derived plan path, while only skip, unskip, and reopen correctly call _plan_file_for_state(). This creates split-brain plan persistence: with a non-default --state path, skip/unskip/reopen correctly modify the co-located plan.json while all other handlers silently read/write the default global plan.json.
claim_distilled: Plan handlers have split-brain plan persistence: skip/unskip/reopen scope to --state path but all other handlers always use the global default plan.json.
claim_restated: 19 plan subcommand call sites use bare load_plan() (resolving to PLAN_FILE, the global default) while cmd_plan_skip, cmd_plan_unskip, and cmd_plan_reopen correctly derive the plan path via plan_path_for_state(runtime.state_path). When a user passes --state pointing to a non-default directory, the three correct handlers load/save to the co-located plan.json while the remaining handlers silently operate on the default .desloppify/plan.json — producing inconsistent plan state across a single command session.

claude.reasoning: Independently verified against code. At time of submission: reorder_handlers.py, queue_render.py, commit_log_handlers.py, cmd.py (_cmd_plan_show, _cmd_plan_reset), cluster_handlers.py (9 handlers), and override_handlers.py (describe, note) all called bare load_plan() with no path argument, defaulting to PLAN_FILE. Meanwhile cmd_plan_skip (override_handlers.py), cmd_plan_unskip, and cmd_plan_reopen used _plan_file_for_state(runtime.state_path) — the correct pattern. The split is confirmed. Divergence only occurs with non-default --state paths; default usage is unaffected. Fix: all handlers now pass plan_path_for_state(runtime.state_path) to load_plan() — confirmed in current code.

why_poor_engineering: Helper function _plan_file_for_state() was defined in override_handlers.py but never extracted to a shared utility or applied to any sibling handlers. 16+ handlers across 6 files were written after the correct pattern was established without awareness of or adherence to it. The result is an asymmetric invariant: three handlers (skip/unskip/reopen) scope plan reads/writes to the --state-derived path while every other handler silently targets the global default — a split-brain with no error, no warning, and no indication to the user.

why_missed.pattern_not_shared: The correct pattern (_plan_file_for_state) was defined inside override_handlers.py as a private helper and never extracted to a shared utility module. Handlers in other files had no access to it and no signal that it existed.
why_missed.no_cross_state_tests: No test exercised a non-default --state path across multiple handler files simultaneously. All tests used the default plan path, so the divergence between skip and reorder/cluster/queue/commit-log was never observed.
why_missed.silent_failure: With the default --state the bug is invisible: both load_plan() and load_plan(plan_path_for_state(default_state)) resolve to the same file. Divergence only manifests with a non-default --state path, and even then produces no error — only silently wrong plan state.

notes: Issue was real and systematic at time of submission. Practical blast radius limited to users with non-default --state configurations, but the pattern was a genuine footgun. Fix complete across all plan subcommand handlers in PR #406 (describe/note/reorder/queue/commit-log/show/reset/9 cluster handlers) and PR #407 (move, resolve, focus, scan_gate, reflect, organize, record_triage_stage). No code changes needed in this PR — fix already landed in main.
